source.multipart_basis: Sample prompt explicitly lists A-frames, ridge pole, eave plates, battens, covering, and anchors as distinct assembled members joined by lashings/bolts; part-family speculation follows IFRC brief's bamboo shelter bill-of-materials.

full_prompt: Design one representative single-family emergency A-frame shelter compliant with the IFRC Emergency Shelter Cluster Technical Brief for bamboo/timber shelters (2023), delivered AS A MULTI-PART ASSEMBLY of A-frame culms, ridge pole, eave plates, roof battens, sheet/tarpaulin covering, and corner ground anchors. The shelter footprint is 4.5 m (L) x 3.5 m (W) in plan (~16 m2 floor area, Sphere-exceeding 4.5 m2/person for a family of 3-4), span direction 3.5 m between eaves, eave height 2.0 m, ridge height 3.2 m. Structural system is two pairs of 3-leg bamboo/timber A-frames at 1.5 m and 3.0 m from the gable end (six A-frame culms total per pair x 2 pairs), plus one ridge pole and two eave plates tying the frames together. Members are 100 mm diameter seasoned bamboo culms (alternatively 75 x 75 mm sawn softwood timber) with the published lower-bound design stresses: bamboo allowable bending 10 MPa, allowable compression along grain 12 MPa, allowable shear 1.2 MPa (timber equivalents per IFRC brief). Roof covering is corrugated metal sheet or tarpaulin on timber battens spaced 600 mm (approximately 8-10 battens across the 4.5 m length). Design loads per IFRC brief: wind speed 120 km/h (33.3 m/s) giving ASCE 7 / Eurocode-simplified dynamic pressure q = 0.613 * V^2 = 0.613 * 33.3^2 ~ 680 Pa on vertical surfaces with cp,lee ~ 0.7 and cp,wind ~ 0.8; snow load 0.5 kN/m2 (or equivalent tropical alternative 0.25 kN/m2 where snow is not applicable); pedestrian / maintenance live 0.5 kN/m2 on roof. The shelter is anchored to ground with four corner anchors sized to resist wind uplift with a safety factor of 1.5. Joinery: culm-to-ridge and culm-to-eave-plate connections are nylon/polyester lashings or M10 galvanized carriage bolts; batten-to-rafter is 80 mm galvanized nails; covering is self-drilling roofing screws to battens. Deliverable is a CAD assembly of the A-frames, ridge pole, eave plates, battens, covering, and anchors suitable for linear static FEA under three independent IFRC load combinations (LC1 wind + dead, LC2 snow + dead, LC3 wind + snow + dead) with the assembled-model check as primary FEM gate. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required.
short_prompt:
Design an IFRC (2023) emergency-shelter bamboo+tarpaulin assembly (ridge pole, rafters, wall studs, sill, tarp cladding, guy ropes, steel ground anchors).

Plan 4.5 x 3.5 m; span 3.5 m; eave 2.0 m; ridge 3.2 m. Bamboo allowables Fb 10 MPa, Fc 12 MPa, Fv 1.2 MPa.

Load cases: LC1 design wind 120 km/h (33.3 m/s, 680 Pa static equivalent); LC2 snow 0.5 kN/m2; LC3 occupant self-weight.

Pass/fail: bamboo member utilisation <= 1.0 against Fb/Fc/Fv; ground-anchor uplift SF >= 1.5; FEM at assembled level; lashing and bolt DCR <= 1.0.

prompt.title: IFRC emergency A-frame family shelter (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 4.5 x 3.5 m bamboo/timber A-frame shelter assembly surviving 120 km/h wind + 0.5 kN/m2 snow on a 3.5 m span; assembled_only FEM gate over A-frames + ridge + eave plates + battens + covering + anchors.

parts.role: primary compression/bending members forming each A-frame pair

geometric_constraints.wall_or_solid: hollow bamboo culm (solid softwood equivalent 75 x 75 mm)

parts.notes: Six culms total across two A-frame pairs (three per pair: two rafters + one tie/collar).

parts.role: longitudinal ridge tying the A-frame pairs at the apex
parts.notes: Single 4.5 m span + end bearings; supports battens and sheet covering.

parts.role: longitudinal eave tie between A-frame feet on each long side
parts.notes: One per long side, bearing on corner anchors.

parts.role: roof batten carrying covering to rafters
parts.notes: Approx. 8 battens per slope x 2 slopes = 16.

parts.role: corrugated metal sheet or tarpaulin panels spanning between battens
parts.notes: Eight standard sheet modules to clad ~17 m2 of roof surface.

parts.role: ground anchor resisting uplift and shear at each shelter corner
parts.notes: Four corner anchors per IFRC brief, designed for SF 1.5 against uplift.

covering_sheet.type: corrugated galvanized steel sheet OR reinforced tarpaulin

load_cases.name: dead_plus_maintenance
load_cases.application: self-weight + 0.5 kN/m2 maintenance live on roof

boundary_conditions.anchors: 4 corner anchors resisting uplift with SF 1.5

objective.primary: pass IFRC criteria on the assembled model

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: assembled_only FEM gate runs LC1/LC2/LC3 on the full multi-part shelter model; R1-R5 must pass together on the assembled geometry
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.detail: rafter apex lashed or M10 carriage-bolted to ridge pole
interfaces.load_path: roof gravity and wind uplift transferred from rafters into ridge

interfaces.detail: rafter foot lashed or bolted to eave plate
interfaces.load_path: rafter thrust into eave plate and then into corner anchors

interfaces.detail: battens nailed across rafters with 80 mm galvanized nails
interfaces.load_path: covering loads transmitted from battens into rafters

interfaces.detail: self-drilling roofing screws through covering into battens
interfaces.load_path: wind uplift and snow gravity from covering into battens

interfaces.detail: eave plate bearing onto driven stake/dead-man anchor, pinned connection
interfaces.load_path: vertical reaction and uplift resistance transferred to ground

fem_check_policy.rationale: IFRC brief evaluates the shelter holistically under combined load cases; stresses in culms, battens, and anchor reactions are only meaningful on the assembled frame+cover+anchor system.

uncertainty_flags.note: Count minima speculated from IFRC bamboo-shelter bill-of-materials; field-built shelters vary in batten density and anchor count.

eval_coverage: wind + snow + maintenance combined FEA on assembled shelter
bamboo-specific allowable-stress design across culms, ridge, eaves, battens
anchor uplift SF at corner anchors
humanitarian emergency shelter multi-part assembly scope

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
